text:
// MAJOR DELTARUNE SPOILERS AHEAD
// MAJOR DELTARUNE SPOILERS AHEAD

I haven't seen anyone theorize about this before:

Why did Sans introduce himself as "sans. sans the skeleton" in undertale?

No one else introduced themselves as "X the monster" and the first thing we got told in undertale is that two races exist, "Monsters, and Humans."

There's a common theme across all Monsters in undertale/Lightners in DELTARUNE, and that is "they're based on alive beings", this is not a rule that it pointed out to us, at least not yet, but the pattern seems pretty consistent at least to me: Lightners are alive, conscious beings that we can reference from real life. Darkners are not, they're objects and that is why they can only exist in the Dark World, this is where their consciousness stays even if they leave...

Now, "Sans the Skeleton", is not an alive or conscious being, or at least he isn't anymore and shouldn't be based on the patterns and explanations we've seen so far, 'it' is an object that once was someone.. someone who knows and keeps reminding himself to "not forget"
Same for Papyrus, they're the "Skeleton Brothers" per their words, 

The pattern that's been consistent to us is that unconscious objects become conscious in the dark world; they get a consciousness, and the Dark World becomes their home, and in the case of the flowers in Chapter 5 they didn't forget what happened to them in the Light world even before they ever go to the Dark World, Flowery explains this to us, in great detail, he remembers every conversation Asgore had with him, and this is what made this theory way more plausible for me;

The rule is slightly different for Flowers than any other Darkners, why? Well, Flowers are living organisms, unlike the other inanimate objects we've seen become Darkners in the Dark World, and in real life, there's a lot of research to support that plants are in fact alive and even respond to stress and being injured similar to how other living organisms do

But how does sans, an unconscious object, that isn't supposed to feel anything in this state, have a consciousness in the light world? What is he supposed to "not forget"?, remember, this is not our story, from the start, this is not about us as the player, we got inserted into it through the program in Deltarune.. and Kris seems to be awfully aware of it to a certain degree from the start

Let's think about it this way for the sake of the theory: This is sans' story, let's assume that he is the true 'protagonist' of it and he once was a human, he doesn't remember what happened throughout all the time he was dead, all the time it took for him to turn into a Skeleton but not decompose for some reason, the Skeleton side of Sans we know is actually from the Dark World, and the undertale picture where he wrote don't forget would add up well enough that if it were a picture of him from back then, he has vague memories about it, much like a normal human that lives for a long time and forgets stuff as they live on, he DID forget, and it all seems so different to him now, he wants to remember what was so important that he wasn't supposed to forget.. And that is why he might've went to extreme lengths no one else went through to figure out a way to leave the Dark World, he is driven by these past memories unlike any other Darkner, the most similar Darkner to him that we know of who also has had a drive to visit the Light World, is Ralsei...

We know Ralsei has been expressing wanting to visit the light world too, but for some reason that we don't yet know, he already gave up trying and just accepted that he won't be able to, he seems to blame it on the prophecy, and that he can't change it, what if he just didn't have that same motivation that sans had to get to the solution he did, maybe we, not just as the player, but together with Kris and Susie, give Ralsei the motivation he needs to stop listening to the prophecy and change it and bring him across to the Light World, what if this is about making us a part of their story when we weren't supposed to be there and this is why our actions do matter in a way, but that's assuming a lot, what's factual is that Ralsei, much like sans, knows more than he lets on, but he thinks it's pointless if he explains it to us, he's figured out a lot of things like travelling from Dark World to Dark World, or not turning into stone like other Darkners, he made clear progress and it sure took a lot out of him, but in the end, he didn't succeed, and he gave up...

We already know from Undertale that sans has a "machine", and he's very well aware of a lot of things that no game character could be aware of, remember what things he brought up specifically in undertale? 'SAVE'ing, 'RESET'ing, 'going back', etc. Can we really put it past him that his Skeleton side is a Darkner, but unlike any other Darkner, he has vague memories of a time where he once was a human before the first Dark World he went into was created, or in other words, before he first became a Darkner - Did he inevitably figure out how to come to the light world because giving up and living in the Dark World was simply not an option for him? Is this motivation what made him become so aware to begin with?

And here's another layer to this that I also thought about a lot: We never actually see Papyrus in Deltarune at all, or ever go into their house.. what if, Papyrus is still stuck in the Dark World when/where Deltarune takes place, and only Sans was able to make it across to the Light World for some reason, it would support why his sprite is colorless in the Light world inside the text boxes (attached image) like a Darkner, he had also asked Kris in Chapter 1 to help his brother with something the next day, then he backtracked when we, the player, tried to ask about it the next day or tried calling about it the same day, he had no interest in speaking to us, he wanted Kris. 

What if, he wanted to ask Kris to help Papyrus in the Dark World or at least be there for him in some way without us knowing? Better yet, what if Kris already met up with Papyrus inside the Dark World in the Skeleton Brothers' house after putting us, the 'SOUL' away, it would support the theory that Papyrus IS the Knight too, I'm unsure what the objective is here, whether it's to bring Papyrus from the Dark World, stop the roaring, or cause the roaring because it has to do with something we don't have enough proof to speculate about reasonably yet, It connects a lot more than this imo and there's way more layers to it but this is the most important stuff I noticed

I'm not a theorist by any means nor am I up to date with every theory and something might already exist that contradicts this, but this all added up way too well and made too much sense for me to keep only to myself because I hadn't seen it get discussed by anyone from that angle, what do you guys think?

I BUILT AN AI FASHION MODEL FROM ONE PHOTO. IT HIT $11K IN 6 WEEKS.

INTRO

Swimwear is seasonal. AI is not.

That single line is why this worked.

I spent six weeks building a fictional AI fashion model from scratch. One character, one face, one wardrobe system. She has 40 outfits and zero opinions about call time. She never asks for a reshoot. She never drifts between frames.

I turned her into a content pipeline that resort and swimwear brands actually pay for. Not because she looks pretty. Pretty is everywhere now. Because she ships 40 looks a week and the face stays locked from frame one to frame last.

This is exactly how the pipeline works, what every node does, and where the money sits.

Here's the exact breakdown:

→ Claude writes a 28-parameter character DNA file in JSON before a single pixel gets rendered. Bone structure ratios, under-eye micro-texture map, hair strand density, skin undertone hex values, nail shape, ear lobe geometry. If the character is not locked at the skeleton level first the face drifts by frame 4 every time.

→ Flux 1.1 Pro generates the base image from that DNA plus a Pinterest moodboard reference. Not a random prompt. A structured seed with locked aspect ratio, camera focal length, and a negative prompt list that runs 38 exclusion terms. No plastic skin, no CGI glow, no symmetry artifacts, no doll face, no synthetic rim lighting, no AI hands.

→ 3 separate Gemini 2.5 Pro nodes run isolated face-zone corrections on the raw output. Lip geometry and moisture. Nose pore density and bridge shadow. Eye capillary mapping and iris depth. Each zone gets its own pass because a single model cannot hold all three consistent in one generation.

→ Magnific AI upscales and merges those corrected zones back onto the base render into one photorealistic layer. This is where the skin stops looking like a render and starts looking like a photo taken on an overcast Tuesday with an iPhone 15 Pro Max.

→ Kling 2.6 animates that locked still into a 5-second video clip but only after the prompt architecture is set. Camera motion path, body movement tempo, wind direction on hair and fabric. You cannot animate before you fix the skin. Skip the texture pass and the face slides around and the whole thing collapses into a deepfake from 2021.

→ ElevenLabs clones a custom voice from a 90-second sample and generates a voiceover that matches the character's visual age and energy. Not a narrator voice. A voice that sounds like it belongs to that face.

→ Topaz Video AI 5 upscales the final output to 4K and stabilizes micro-jitter on the jaw and collarbone, then CapCut drops it into a pre-built brand template with product tags and platform-native captions. 19 finished videos upload to TikTok Studio in one batch because the brand voice was pre-programmed in the character DNA file from step one.

[VIDEO INSERT / text label: TONE / 0:04]

The key move 96% of people skip: you cannot animate the photo before you correct the skin texture at the zone level.

Everyone rushes straight from the base render into image-to-video. The face warps on the second frame. The lips melt into the chin. The eyes lose depth and start tracking like a painted mask. Three seconds in and the viewer already knows it is AI and scrolls past.

The system isolates lip geometry, nose pore density, and iris depth into separate correction passes before the avatar ever moves. That one step is the difference between content that looks generated and content that looks shot on location.

Swimwear brands now pay $750 for the initial character build plus a full week of content, and $249 a month to keep that AI model as a permanent roster asset they redeploy every season without a single new photoshoot.

48 hours from brief to finished lookbook. Traditional shoot takes 6 weeks. The brands figured out which one scales.

How to use Claude in 25 different ways:

(takes 60 seconds to read, saves you 60 hours)

1. Bookmark this image. You'll need it tomorrow.
2. Full guide + prompts at: https://t.co/CMWuI4VO4N.
3. Don't pay anything. It's free in the email.

---

1. ASK → Chat
☑️ Quick questions. Quick answers.
☑️ Don't use Cowork for "write a good email?"

2. THINK → Opus 4.8
☑️ The smartest model. Always switch to it.
☑️ For deep work, complex writing & thinking.

3. SPEED → Sonnet 4.6
☑️ Faster, cheaper, still excellent.
☑️ Use it for grammar, summaries, short answers.

4. REASON → Extended Thinking
☑️ Toggle it on for hard problems.
☑️ Turn it off for simple ones (saves tokens).

5. BUILD → Cowork
☑️ Where serious work happens. .docx, .xlsx, .pptx.
☑️ Point it at a folder. It works like an employee.

6. SHIP → Claude Code
☑️ For developers (and curious non-devs).
☑️ Build websites and apps from your terminal.

7. MODEL → Claude in Excel
☑️ Install the add-in from the Excel store.
☑️ Real formulas, real models, no copy-paste.

8. SLIDES → Gamma connector
☑️ One prompt. Full presentation. With research.
☑️ Connect Gamma inside Claude.

9. MOCKUP → Claude Design
☑️ Go to claude .ai/design. Build websites in 1 hour.
☑️ Upload a DESIGN .md. Export to Canva, PDF.

10. BROWSE → Claude in Chrome
☑️ A browsing agent. Reads any page for you.
☑️ Acts inside the browser, not just talks about it.

11. CLICK → Computer Use
☑️ Settings > Turn it on.
☑️ Claude clicks, types, browses on your Mac.

12. REMOTE → Dispatch
☑️ Connect your phone. Text a task.
☑️ It works on your computer while you're away.

13. SEARCH → Web Search
☑️ Click the '+'. turn it on.
☑️ It reads the live web before answering.

14. INVESTIGATE → Research
☑️ For deep multi-source reports.
☑️ 13 minutes. 50+ sources. One markdown file.

15. TRIGGER → Skills
☑️ "Use the skill-creator to build a skill for [task]."
☑️ Type /skill. It fires automatically.

16. STACK → Plugins
☑️ Cowork > Customize > Browse Plugins.
☑️ Bundles of skills for marketing, legal, sales.

17. PERSIST → Projects
☑️ One Project per recurring deliverable.
☑️ Upload one gold-standard example.

18. AUTOMATE → Scheduled Tasks
☑️ Every Monday at 7am, Claude runs your report.
☑️ Set it up once. No manual work. 

19. CONNECT → Connectors
☑️ Gmail, Drive, Slack, Notion, Granola, Calendar.
☑️ 50+ apps. Claude reads, writes & acts inside.

20. RENDER → Artifacts
☑️ Side pane where Claude opens charts, docs.
☑️ Interactive. Editable. Shareable.

21. VOICE → Custom Styles
☑️ Set "Concise" or build your own.
☑️ Saves you from re-explaining your voice.

22. SHARE → Team Projects
☑️ Same Project. Whole team inside.
☑️ Skills, files, instructions. Shared once.

23. GUARD → Global Instructions
☑️ Settings > Cowork > Global Instructions.
☑️ Tell Claude what to always do.

24. ORGANIZE → Obsidian Vault
☑️ Free app. Opens your Cowork folder neatly.
☑️ Edit files like Google Docs.

25. BRIEF → about-me file.
☑️ One md. file. Under 2,000 words.
☑️ Your style, your rules, your voice

Follow @markstrong_07

THIS GUY BUILT A COMFYUI WORKFLOW THAT TURNS ONE AI GIRL INTO A CONTENT PRODUCT

The video is not just “make a pretty AI girl.”

It shows the real money part:
repeatability.

ComfyUI lets you lock the character, load the model stack, use a reference image, batch prompts, and generate many shots with the same face, style, and vibe.

That is the difference between random AI art and an asset you can sell around.

The simple path:

Pick a niche first.
Fitness girl, fashion girl, gaming girl, luxury travel girl, skincare girl, anime-inspired creator.

Then build a consistent face and visual style in ComfyUI.
Not 1 image.
A bank of 100+ usable posts.

Then monetize the output in one of 4 ways:

1. Sell content packs to small brands that need ad creatives.

2. Build a synthetic influencer page and test affiliate offers.

3. Create custom AI model/look packages for creators who want a character but do not want to learn nodes.

4. Sell the workflow, prompts, and setup as a productized service.

The mistake is thinking the model is the business.

The business is the boring system around it:
consistent character, content calendar, offer, distribution, and proof that the visuals convert.

ComfyUI gives more control than simple AI image apps because the workflow can be repeated, edited, and improved instead of praying for one lucky generation.

But the caveat is big:
an AI girl with no niche, no offer, and no audience is just another folder of renders.

If you want to make money from this, do not start by generating “hot girl” images.

Start by asking:
who pays for this visual attention?
what product can she sell?
what content can be posted every day?
what makes the character recognizable after 3 seconds?

That is where the money is.

What a month it's been!

As our Forget Me Not Appeal 2026 comes to a close, we're looking back on some incredible moments made possible by our supporters, volunteers, partners and campaigners.

Across the month of June, we 👇

- Saw more than 117,000 people proudly wear our Forget Me Not Appeal badge in honour of loved ones affected by dementia

- Joined with 50,000 supporters and @jonsnowC4 to call for better dementia diagnosis through our open letter

- Announced 17 new research grants to help explore dementia's causes, diagnosis, treatment and prevention

- Celebrated the two-year extension of our partnership with The FA

- Raised an amazing £1.5 million to help fund life-changing support and ground-breaking research

- Celebrated our incredible volunteers during Volunteers' Week

- Helped deliver support through over 6,000 calls to our Dementia Support Line

Thank you for making this Forget Me Not Appeal so special and helping us get one step closer to ending the devastation caused by dementia 💙

[Image description: A light blue graphic with a white rounded box with a red heart icon above, that reads: ‘Good news and things to celebrate this month’. Below, a blue forget-me-not flower is surrounded by curved text that reads: ‘Forget Me Not Appeal edition’.]

AI app ideas are cheap. Launchable templates are the bottleneck.

Awesome Generative AI Apps is a curated GitHub repo of 50+ open-source generative AI app templates for builders who want to clone, deploy, and adapt AI SaaS products.

It helps you skip the blank Next.js project by grouping templates for image, video, beauty/fashion, e-commerce, writing, agents, audio, and integrations, with setup and deploy paths already documented.

Key features:

• 50+ app templates – cloneable products across image generation, video generation, virtual try-on, product photography, content, agents, and more
• SaaS plumbing included – README highlights Stripe billing, Google OAuth, Prisma/PostgreSQL, credit balances, and job history
• Vercel deployment path – templates point to Deploy to Vercel buttons and a quick-start flow
• Category navigation – table of contents lets you scan by use case instead of hunting through random demos
• MIT license – repo metadata and README both mark it as MIT licensed for reuse and adaptation

It’s open-source (MIT license).

Link in the reply 👇

Diffusion language models, which generate text by repeatedly filling in masked parts rather than writing strictly from left to right, are becoming a serious alternative to standard transformer language models, but the usual ways of improving reasoning after training were built for left-to-right generation and do not fit the diffusion models.

The authors of this paper introduce a self-training method in which the model first produces its own answer, then uses parts of that completed answer as helpful context while learning how earlier denoising steps should behave.

Instead of comparing teacher and student one next token at a time, it compares them at each denoising step, which matches how diffusion language models actually generate text. 

Across math and planning tasks, d-OPSD matches or beats supervised finetuning and reinforcement-learning baselines, while needing far fewer optimization steps than the main RL baseline.

https://t.co/eRqYj4YPwi

Super excited about open-source router systems and routing models like @vllm_project semantic router: https://t.co/Gwza9jPWzr

The future is multi-models and you'll want to customize your router the same way you customize your code!

It could be the key to tilt the value capture from a few expensive frontier models to a long-tail of models (especially open-source). 

More people should build those!

How can we make RL post-training for robot policies more sample-efficient?

Check out OGPO: an RL post-training method for pre-trained flow-matching and diffusion policies.

OGPO combines off-policy Q-learning with GRPO-style policy updates to enable fast & stable fine-tuning.
